summary: Video set to music with narration. It cuts between black-and-white archival footage of early-1900s crowds, a city skyline and a rail line (one shot under a red color wash with the caption 'JOBS OVERSEAS') and color-graded footage of a factory rally with workers in hard hats behind a blue 'THE GOLDEN AGE' banner and the Department of Labor building draped with a central American flag and two banners reading 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'. All-caps captions read 'HE PROMISED', 'AND BUSINESS', 'JOBS OVERSEAS', 'AND INDUSTRY TOGETHER', 'WHO PUT THE AMERICAN'. The portraits on the two banners are Trump and Theodore Roosevelt. Narration references Roosevelt, a 'square deal', trade deals, jobs sent abroad, and Trump.

script.scene: Black-and-white archival footage of a packed early-1900s crowd at a grandstand around a draped platform; caption 'HE PROMISED'; narration about Roosevelt promising a 'square deal' for the American people.
Black-and-white skyline of an early-20th-century city with signage reading B.T. BABBITT 'BEST SOAP'; caption 'AND BUSINESS'; narration about uniting leaders and unions and using tariff revenue.
Color shot of an overgrown, abandoned railway curve under a heavy red color wash; caption 'JOBS OVERSEAS'; narration about decades of leadership and jobs sent abroad.

Factory or steel-plant rally: rows of workers in orange hard hats and high-visibility clothing behind a large blue 'THE GOLDEN AGE' banner, phones raised, a figure in a suit at right; caption 'AND INDUSTRY TOGETHER'; narration about Trump bringing workers and industry together like Roosevelt.

Wide shot of the U.S. Department of Labor building under a blue sky, draped with a central American flag and two banners showing Trump and Roosevelt portraits reading 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'; caption 'WHO PUT THE AMERICAN'.

notable_text: HE PROMISED / AND BUSINESS / JOBS OVERSEAS / THE GOLDEN AGE / AND INDUSTRY TOGETHER / WHO PUT THE AMERICAN [WORKER FIRST] / banner: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST
